customManagers.description: Track web/buf.gen.yaml BSR codegen plugin pins via their inline `# renovate:` annotations

Track the Go buf.gen.yaml / buf.gen.internal.yaml BSR codegen plugin pins via their inline `# renovate:` annotations (holomush-9vyc3). Separate from the web manager: the patterns are root-anchored (`^buf.gen…`) so they exclude web/buf.gen.yaml and buf.gen.docs.yaml, the datasource is `go` (per-line via the annotation), and the version `v` prefix is captured into currentValue — Renovate's go versioning writes the bump back as `vX.Y.Z`, so capturing after `:v` (as the web manager does) would corrupt the pin to `:vvX.Y.Z`.

packageRules.description: Batch all non-major npm/pnpm bumps into a single PR
NATS packages — extra stabilization time, grouped, weekly schedule
Major version bumps require manual review

Group the web/buf.gen.yaml bufbuild/es pin with its @bufbuild/protoc-gen-es web/package.json devDependency so they bump together in one PR. Not auto-merged — a pin bump doesn't regenerate the committed _pb.ts stubs, so a human runs `task web:generate` on the bump PR (holomush-ai3o5).

Group the Go buf.gen.yaml / buf.gen.internal.yaml BSR codegen plugin pins with their go.mod runtimes so each bumps in lockstep: protocolbuffers/go↔google.golang.org/protobuf, connectrpc/go↔connectrpc.com/connect (both ship the generator in the runtime module, same version); protoc-gen-go-grpc tracks its own google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc submodule, independent of the google.golang.org/grpc runtime. Not auto-merged — a pin bump doesn't regenerate the committed *.pb.go stubs, so a human runs `task proto` on the bump PR. Last in the array so it overrides the broader gomod auto-merge and 'Google Go' grouping for these packages (holomush-9vyc3).
